terminologyProfile.descriptionRule: Use measurable 3D graphics terms. Avoid vague words unless they are paired with concrete geometry/material/shader parameters.

formLanguage: monumental organic trunk with a broad Y-shaped fork
radial branch fans framing a luminous central axis
warm gold and cool cyan bioluminescent accents
constellation-like points and code glyph ornamentation
dense but perforated crown with readable negative spaces
structureKind: curve-swept emissive sap and glyph lines
motionPotential: slow branch sway from root pivots
gold energy pulse along trunk-to-tip paths
materialFamilies: warm dark-brown bark with carved grooves
cyan emissive leaves and constellation nodes
objectClass.notes: The target is the central luminous tree, not the surrounding GitHub village, mountains, people, signs, or star field. Reconstruct it as a stylized real-time hero prop rather than an exact scene copy.

reasoning: The tree occupies the central upper half of the frame and its trunk fork, roots, and main bough flow are readable despite the surrounding village.
The identity depends on multiple coupled systems: broad crown silhouette, tapered branching hierarchy, carved bark, emissive gold paths, mixed amber/cyan foliage, and constellation ornaments.
Rear branches and exact branch depth are ambiguous in the single frontal illustration, so the implementation should use a plausible radial depth distribution rather than claim exact reconstruction.
Repeated leaves and luminous nodes require deterministic instancing and LOD; individual leaves should not become separate draw calls.

specDepthDecision.rationale: A shallow trunk-plus-spheres model would lose the reference identity. The spec needs separate trunk/crown masses, at least three branch levels, repeated leaf and constellation systems, local emissive overlays, and four review viewpoints.

definitionOfDone: At the reference camera, the tree reads as a monumental golden code-tree before the surrounding village is added.
The main trunk rises from a broad root flare, splits into a wide Y, and supports left, center, and right branch fans with visible sky gaps.
Gold energy channels remain bright and spatially attached to bark under neutral and reference lighting without making the bark itself black.
Amber and cyan leaf clusters plus constellation nodes form a luminous crown while preserving the major branch silhouette.
The asset exposes branch pivots, effect sockets, collider proxies, and deterministic repetition systems for animation and Sculpt DNA variants.

featureGroups.name: Forked trunk, root flare, and crown silhouette
qualityCriteria: The trunk is approximately one third of total tree height before the first major fork.
Left and right crown masses span roughly 1.4 times the visible tree height.
Major negative spaces remain visible between the center spire and side branch fans.
failureModes: single vertical cylinder with a spherical canopy
fork starts too high or side crowns collapse inward
dense leaves erase the branch-fan silhouette

featureGroups.name: Radial branch hierarchy and embedded joints
qualityCriteria: Left, center, and right primary boughs emerge from named sockets on the trunk fork.
Secondary branches taper from root to tip and overlap their parent at every joint.
Rear branches add depth without changing the frontal identity.
failureModes: uniform branch radii or evenly spaced radial spokes
all branch levels merged into an inert mesh

featureGroups.name: Gold energy and code-glyph network
qualityCriteria: Warm emissive paths originate near the root core and split along the same hierarchy as the bark branches.
Energy lines remain narrow enough to reveal bark albedo and roughness.
Glyph marks follow branch tangents instead of floating as screen-space text.
failureModes: trunk reads as black because emission is the only visible surface response
glow blooms into a flat white silhouette
code glyphs detach from the curved branch surface

featureGroups.name: Amber and cyan leaf canopy
qualityCriteria: Leaf clusters are distributed along outer and upper branch tips rather than filling the trunk interior.
Amber is dominant while cyan accents occupy a smaller, intentionally uneven share.
Leaf cards vary in orientation, scale, emission, and visibility while preserving deterministic seeds.
failureModes: cyan and amber distributed as uniform noise
leaf density hides all major boughs

featureGroups.name: Constellation crown and hanging light ornaments
qualityCriteria: A small number of cyan and warm-white nodes form readable linked constellations around the upper crown.
Short hanging light strands descend from selected side branches.
Ornaments use effect sockets and instancing instead of being baked into bark textures.
failureModes: random sparkles with no grouped paths

visualDeltaChecks: first-fork height and left/center/right branch-fan angle delta
gold energy width, bloom, and branch-following continuity delta
amber-to-cyan leaf ratio and crown negative-space delta
constellation-node grouping and hanging-light placement delta
bark midtone visibility under neutral and reference lighting delta
antiShallowSpecRules: Do not proceed to code if qualityContract.qualityBar is unassessed.
Do not proceed to code if the spec only contains a root component for a moderate or complex object.
Do not proceed to code if required featureGroups are not represented by componentTree, materials, or repetitionSystems.
Do not proceed to code if visible local features are described only in prose and not attached to components/materials/evidenceRefs.
Do not proceed past structural-pass if attached child parts lack attachment.parentSocket, localStart, localEnd, embedDepth/overlap, and gapTolerance.
Do not pass material look-dev when albedo is reused as roughness, height, normal, or AO.
Do not pass material look-dev without macro, meso, and micro surface frequency bands for close-up materials.
Do not pass reference-fidelity material look-dev from a source image without usable referencePbr maps or an explicit documented limitation.
Do not patch a spec with extracted PBR maps when extraction confidence is below the target threshold unless the user explicitly accepts lower fidelity.
Do not allow foliage or bloom to hide the three primary branch fans.
Do not model the village, GitHub logos, signs, or people as part of the tree asset.
Do not treat the baked gold glow in the illustration as bark albedo; author bark and emission as separate material/effect systems.

mustMatch: broad Y-shaped trunk and three primary branch fans
warm bark remains readable beneath separate gold emission
amber-dominant leaf crown with sparse cyan accents
constellation nodes and hanging lights attached to outer branches
root flare and central energy path remain visible at the reference camera
niceToHave: subtle rear branch depth and parallax
animated energy pulse and leaf shimmer

visualAcceptance.scoringRule: AI vision must inspect a side-by-side reference/render sheet and score the current pass from 0 to 1. Pixel-diff code may assist diagnostics but cannot approve a pass.

featureReviewPolicy.selectionRule: Choose only the most visually salient, identity-defining, user-prioritized, or high-risk semantic systems. Group repeated parts instead of reviewing every mesh. AI vision scores every selected feature from the same full reference/render pair.

specRefineTriggers: local feature not traceable to viewEvidence
codeRefineTriggers: spec is adequate but generated geometry/material does not match
lighting hides geometry or material response
stopCriteria: target fidelity reached or user accepts current approximation
remaining gaps require new reference images or manual art

screenshotPolicy.minimumEvidence: Each visual pass needs a reference image, rendered screenshot, side-by-side comparison sheet, AI vision score, layer scores, and critique before choosing continue.
screenshotPolicy.reviewPairRule: Compare the same camera/viewpoint whenever possible; do not judge a front reference against a random render angle.
screenshotPolicy.acceptanceAuthority: AI vision review of the comparison sheet. Code-generated pixel similarity is not sufficient evidence.

featureReviewTargets.name: Left, center, and right branch fans

sculptPipeline.blockedReason: Sculpt DNA changed visible geometry or material parameters; this variant needs fresh browser and AI-vision evidence before deeper passes unlock.
nextRequiredEvidence: fresh blockout browser screenshot for this Sculpt DNA variant
side-by-side comparison against the intended family reference or design target
AI vision layer and semantic feature scores for this variant

referencePbrExtraction.acceptedLimitation: The stylized source bakes bloom, emission, and scene lighting into the pixels. Use procedural bark and emissive materials; treat any extraction only as palette evidence.

mustAvoid: plastic-looking smooth bark, stone, cloth, foliage, or aged material
local color/detail described only in prose without material masks
claiming exact PBR recovery when confidence is below the target threshold
reference lighting copied without separating material readability

screenshotReview: Compare albedo palette and local color zones.
Compare cavity dirt, edge wear, stains, moss, scratches, or other local masks.
Compare key/fill/rim structure, exposure, tone mapping, background, and contact shadows.
Capture a neutral-light render to verify material readability without reference lighting.
Capture a grazing-light close-up to expose flat normals, uniform roughness, tiling, and plastic highlights.
Capture a reference-matched render from the same camera framing as the source.

actionReadiness.contract: Every macro/meso component should be generated as a stable named Object3D pivot node with a mesh child, action metadata, optional sockets, collider proxy, and destruction metadata.
requiredComponentFields: attachment for child appendages, connectors, limbs, tubes, handles, legs, horns, wings, branches, or cables
authoringRules: Do not collapse independently movable parts into one mesh.
Put transforms on component pivot groups, not only on raw meshes.
For attached child parts, put the pivot at the semantic root/socket and build visible geometry from localStart to localEnd.
Represent hinge, socket, detachable, and breakable intent even when no animation is implemented yet.
Use simplified collider proxies for runtime physics instead of visual mesh colliders by default.

destructionPolicy.fractureGroupNaming: Use stable semantic names such as body-shell, left-hinge, glass-panel, branch-segment.
destructionPolicy.debrisStrategy: Prefer detachable component groups and a small number of procedural fragments over random mesh explosion.

assumptions: Only the central tree is reconstructed; village buildings, logos, text signs, people, mountains, and sky are excluded.
Rear branch depth is inferred as a shallow radial fan because the source provides one frontal illustration.
Small branch text is treated as abstract code-like glyph marks rather than exact readable copy.
Amber foliage occupies about 75 percent of emissive leaves and cyan foliage about 25 percent.
The final prop is stylized and browser-real-time; it does not claim botanical or physical-lighting accuracy.

coordinateFrame.up: +Y follows the trunk from root flare to crown
coordinateFrame.scaleReference: 12 world units from ground contact to highest branch tip

silhouette.boundingShape: wide luminous crown over a tapered trunk; crown width about 1.4 times total tree height
aspectRatios: first major fork at 4.2 units above ground
silhouette.symmetry: approximately bilateral at macro scale with intentionally asymmetric branch and leaf distribution
dominantCurves: trunk centerline bends slightly right before splitting into a broad Y
left and right primary boughs arc upward and outward
center spire narrows through two fork levels
root buttresses sweep outward and down into the ground
negativeSpaces: inverted triangular gap between left primary bough and center spire
smaller vertical gap between center and right primary bough
perforated crown edge with star-field visible between leaf clusters

observations: Tree dominates the central frame and extends from village ground to near the top edge.
Crown is wider than it is tall and uses warm gold as the dominant luminous color.
Surrounding village elements overlap only the lowest roots and should not be included.
Warm brown trunk remains visible between bright gold channels.
Primary fork opens around one third of the tree height.
Fine code-like marks follow branch tangents.
Amber leaves dominate upper branch tips.
Cyan nodes and short connecting lines form sparse constellation groups.
Leaf density remains low enough to expose the branch network.
Left crown sweeps farther horizontally than the right.
Cyan constellation accents cluster near the outer upper-left branches.
Several short warm hanging-light strands descend below the outer bough.
Right crown is slightly more upright and compact.
Cyan leaf accents are concentrated near upper-right tips.
Gold branch emission traces the inner edge of major boughs.
Root flare spreads into at least four visible buttresses.
The brightest energy source sits at the root center and fades upward.
Village paths occlude some root tips, so hidden continuation is inferred.

geometryDescriptor.topologyIntent: tapered trunk with enough radial and height segments for root flare and carved energy channels
geometryDescriptor.uvStrategy: cylindrical projection with world-scale bark continuity
geometryDescriptor.normalStrategy: smooth vertex normals plus independent bark normal map

constraints: root pivot remains at ground contact

surfaceDetail.normalPattern: vertical bark plates with fork-following flow
surfaceDetail.occlusionPattern: dark branch sockets and deep bark fissures
surfaceDetail.notes: Bark must retain warm midtones beneath emission.

geometryDescriptor.topologyIntent: temporary perforated blockout mass later replaced by instanced leaves

surfaceDetail.notes: Blockout material remains translucent enough to show branch gaps.

geometryDescriptor.topologyIntent: temporary perforated blockout mass later replaced by instanced leaves

localFeatures.size: roughly 20 percent of right crown leaves

surfaceDetail.notes: Keep right crown slightly more compact than left.

geometryDescriptor.topologyIntent: root-to-tip tapered branch with curved centerline refinement
root-to-tip tapered branch with curved centerline refinement
tall tapered center branch with two implied sub-forks

componentTree.name: Central gold energy conduit
deformations: fork into primary branches during form refinement

surfaceDetail.notes: Emission intensity decreases from root to crown and must not flatten bark contrast.

materials.name: Warm carved bark

albedo.samplingNotes: Preserve warm brown midtones; do not sample the darkest baked shadows as base albedo.

colorVariation.pattern: vertical bark plates and warm ridge tops

textureProjection.texelDensityIntent: 0.25 world-unit bark plates remain consistent across trunk and branch taper.

surfaceFrequencyBands.role: broad trunk value and root-flare breakup

roughness.localResponse: rough cavities, smoother warm ridge tops, slightly polished energy-channel edges

ambientOcclusion.notes: Concentrate AO inside branch sockets, root contacts, and carved grooves.

localOverrides.changes: raise albedo warmth and lower roughness by 0.08 near gold core
deepen AO and darken albedo without reaching black

shaderNotes: Keep bark and gold emission separate.
Use warm fill light so shaded bark remains above near-black.
Use triplanar fallback at branch forks to avoid cylindrical seams.
materials.notes: The prior dark-tree failure is explicitly rejected: bark must remain readable under neutral lighting.

materials.name: Golden code energy

albedo.samplingNotes: Core approaches warm white; edges remain orange-gold.

textureProjection.texelDensityIntent: pulse spacing remains stable along each spline.

ambientOcclusion.notes: Energy is self-luminous; avoid dirty AO.

localOverrides.changes: raise emission to warm white and broaden halo
reduce emission by 35 percent and narrow path width

shaderNotes: Use additive bloom only after tone mapping is stable.
Clamp bloom so branch and bark edges remain readable.
Animate pulse in material state rather than changing geometry.

materials.name: Amber luminous leaves
materials.shaderModel: MeshPhysicalMaterial with alpha cards or thin extruded leaves

albedo.samplingNotes: Warm amber dominates the crown with pale gold tips.

textureProjection.texelDensityIntent: one leaf texture per card with deterministic atlas rotation.

roughness.localResponse: smoother central vein and brighter thin edges

normal.pattern: central vein and shallow side veins

ambientOcclusion.notes: Cluster AO only; leaf faces remain luminous.

localOverrides.changes: shift toward pale gold and raise emission

shaderNotes: Limit overdraw with alpha cutoff and LOD.

materials.name: Cyan constellation leaves
materials.shaderModel: MeshPhysicalMaterial with emissive thin cards and point-node companions

albedo.samplingNotes: Cyan remains a sparse accent rather than replacing amber.

textureProjection.texelDensityIntent: one leaf texture per card with deterministic atlas rotation.

ambientOcclusion.notes: Keep cyan accents bright and clean.

localOverrides.changes: replace leaf card with brighter sphere node and thin connecting segment

shaderNotes: Keep cyan coverage under one quarter of luminous foliage.
Use a separate seed from amber leaves.
Group nodes into semantic constellations rather than uniform random scatter.

sculptDNA.authoringRule: These controls are conservative starters. Rename them in object language, add coupling groups for dependent proportions, and add ratio/range constraints before generating a production family.

repetitionSystems.name: Amber leaf clusters
repetitionSystems.distribution: branch-tip weighted with 18 percent positional jitter and 35 percent scale variation

repetitionSystems.name: Cyan leaf accent clusters
repetitionSystems.distribution: clustered blue-noise near outer and upper tips

repetitionSystems.name: Constellation nodes and links
repetitionSystems.distribution: six semantic groups with two to seven nodes each
constraints: connect only nodes in the same group
attach group roots to branch sockets

repetitionSystems.name: Warm hanging light strands
repetitionSystems.distribution: selected lower branch sockets with 0.4-1.2 unit drop

buildPasses.goal: Match the root flare, first-fork height, and wide left/right crown silhouette without relying on bloom.
acceptance: Trunk height, crown width, and first-fork placement match the reference-front evidence.
Bark blockout remains warm brown rather than black.
AI vision silhouette and root-flare targets pass.

buildPasses.goal: Build the three primary branch fans, outer/rear branches, buttress roots, and central energy conduit.
acceptance: All branch and root attachments overlap named sockets with no visible gaps.
Left, center, and right branch fans preserve the reference negative spaces.
Constellation and hanging-light systems have named effect sockets.
AI vision branch-hierarchy target passes.

buildPasses.goal: Replace straight branch cylinders with tapered curved sweeps, root-flare deformation, and branch-surface glyph channels.
acceptance: Primary boughs curve and taper continuously from embedded roots to tips.
Root buttresses contact the ground plane.
Gold channels and abstract glyph grooves follow surface tangents.
AI vision root-flare and branch-form targets pass.

buildPasses.goal: Establish readable warm bark, controlled gold emission, amber foliage, and sparse cyan accents.
acceptance: Bark retains visible warm midtones under neutral lighting.
Gold emission is authored separately and does not flatten bark into a black silhouette.
Amber remains dominant and cyan coverage stays below one quarter of luminous foliage.
Independent roughness, normal, AO, and emissive responses are present.
AI vision bark, energy, and dual-color canopy targets pass.

buildPasses.goal: Add bark plates, carved channels, deterministic leaf cards, constellation links, and hanging-light strands.
acceptance: Bark detail survives grazing light without obvious tiling.
Instanced leaves preserve branch visibility and named negative spaces.
Constellation groups and hanging lights remain branch-attached.
AI vision energy and canopy targets pass.

buildPasses.goal: Balance neutral material readability with the moonlit cyan-and-gold reference mood.
acceptance: Warm bark reads in neutral, grazing, and reference-match views.
Bloom preserves gold path width and leaf silhouettes.
Cool environment rim separates outer crown edges.
Ground contact shadow anchors the root flare.
AI vision bark, energy, and ornament targets pass.

buildPasses.goal: Expose branch sway pivots, energy pulse sockets, leaf shimmer controls, colliders, and semantic fracture groups.
acceptance: Primary branches rotate from fork sockets.
Energy pulse travels from root origin through named split sockets.
Leaf and ornament visibility/material states are independently controllable.
Collider proxies do not use high-detail visual meshes.
AI vision and runtime metadata review pass.

buildPasses.goal: Instance repeated foliage and ornaments, add LODs, and keep the hero silhouette within the browser budget.
acceptance: Near LOD stays under 180k triangles and 90 draw calls.
Far LOD preserves trunk fork, crown width, and gold energy read.
Repeated leaves, nodes, and hanging lights use instancing or batched geometry.
Target 45 FPS at hero view and 60 FPS at far LOD.

lodPlan.strategy: full component tree and material layers
merge static components and reduce local feature geometry

performanceBudget.optimizationPolicy: Preserve the fork silhouette, bark midtones, gold energy continuity, and dual-color canopy before reducing hidden branch or leaf detail.

lightingFromPhoto: Key light: warm gold emissive contribution from root and branch channels, strongest near the trunk center; use emission for glow but not as the only bark illumination.
Fill light: soft cool-blue hemisphere fill from the night sky at low intensity so shaded bark remains warm brown instead of black.
Rim/environment light: cyan-blue back and side rim from the star field to separate outer leaves and branch tips.
Exposure and tone mapping: ACES Filmic tone mapping with exposure near 1.0; tune bloom threshold above bark values and below gold/cyan emission peaks.
Background: deep navy-to-indigo gradient or environment map with restrained stars; keep background luminance below the tree crown.
Contact shadow: soft ground shadow and local AO under the root flare, branch sockets, and overlapping leaf clusters.
proceduralStrategy: Block out the trunk, first fork, and two crown masses before enabling any emission.
Generate every branch from attachment localStart to localEnd with taper and parent overlap.
Replace straight structural cylinders with deterministic Catmull-Rom or cubic Bezier curve sweeps during form refinement.
Keep bark PBR and gold emissive channels independent so neutral lighting can reveal the wood surface.
Instance amber leaves, cyan accents, constellation nodes, and hanging lights with separate deterministic seeds.
Reserve cyan accents for grouped identity landmarks; do not distribute them as uniform random noise.
Drive energy pulses through named root, fork, and branch-tip sockets.
Optimize repetition and rear depth only after reference-front silhouette and material readability pass.
animationAnchors: root base pivot supports whole-tree placement and slow global sway
left-fork, right-fork, and center-spire sockets drive independent primary branch sway
pulse-origin and pulse-split sockets drive root-to-crown energy animation
left-lights and right-lights sockets anchor hanging ornament strands
leaf repetition systems expose seeded shimmer, visibility, and emission phase controls
destructionAnchors: primary branch fracture groups use named fork seams rather than random whole-tree explosion
outer branches can detach without merging crown repetition buffers into the trunk
root system and central energy conduit remain non-breakable by default
leaf and constellation debris use lightweight instanced fragments
risks: Single frontal artwork does not reveal exact rear branch topology.
Baked bloom and scene lighting prevent exact physical material extraction.
Small branch glyphs are not reliably legible and are treated as abstract code-like marks.
Dense transparent leaf cards can create overdraw unless instanced and LOD-controlled.
Reference village scale is illustrative; final world-unit scale must be calibrated in the target scene.
